title: The Five Loaves
subtitle: Christ feeds five thousand in the desert place

stillness: He went up into the mountain.
He teaches you to rest from the tumults
and from the turmoil that is in the world.
Sit quietly with Him a while, and be still.
gospel: When Jesus had heard of the death of John,
He withdrew from there by boat
into a desert place apart, near Tiberias.
And the multitudes, hearing of it, followed Him —
the simple and the lowly and the poor,
not on beasts or in carriages,
but on foot, by the labor of their own feet.
Now the Pasch was nigh, the festival day of the Jews.
When the evening was come, His disciples came, saying:
the hour to return home and to eat is already past.
But there is here one boy
who has five barley loaves and two fishes;
yet what are these among so many?
And Jesus said: Make the men sit down
upon the grass, the green grass of the field.
So the men sat down, in number about five thousand,
besides the little ones and the women.
Then Jesus took the loaves and the fishes
into His glorious and venerable hands,
and looking up to heaven, He gave thanks,
and blessed them, that by His blessing they might be multiplied.
behold: Attend now to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the multitude, famished, waiting for Him until evening —
so greatly did they long to hear Christ
that they forgot to bring food with them.
Behold the Lord lifting up His eyes —
not casting them here and there,
but seated modestly, attentive among His disciples.
He came down from the mountain to meet them,
Behold the one boy, and his five barley loaves —
things held in contempt, poor things.
Behold the hands that have the power to multiply,
and the eyes that look first to heaven before the gift is given.
consider: He had mercy on them, in body and in soul.
Whomever He healed in the body, He likewise renewed in the soul.
Four kindnesses the Lord did for those who followed Him:
He received the weary, He taught the ignorant,
He healed the sick, He fed the hungry.
And four He does in you who follow Him:
He receives those wearied by the works of their sins,
He gives light to those blinded by the darkness of sin,
He heals those wounded by the darts of sin,
He refreshes those weighed down by the burden of their sins.
But no one receives the food of Christ unless first he is healed;
for after the remission of sins the heavenly nourishment is given.
Learn from the disciples: though they had little, they gave to the needy.
Bidden to bring the five loaves, they did not say,
from where shall we ease our own hunger?
Earthly things were of small account to them;
they were possessed by things of the spirit.
prayer: make me lift the eyes of my mind from carnal delights,
from earthly riches and worldly ambitions, up to You,
and make me sit down upon the grass
of fleshly pleasure, of passing possession, of worldly glory,
that I may be worthy to be filled
with the refreshment of the five spiritual loaves:
the fear of the divine judgment, the horror of sin,
the sorrow of contrition, the shame of confession,
and the desire to change and grow toward the better —
which one boy, namely humility, holds,
the humility that is wont to merit grace and glory.

latinJewel.latin: Tantum enim desiderabant audire Christum, quod omiserant apportare secum alimentum; propter quod necessarium erat fieri miraculum.
latinJewel.english: So greatly did they long to hear Christ that they had forgotten to bring food with them; and for that reason a miracle had to be wrought.
